partyscape 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • Core workflows
    • Profile crossings: when ENP isn't enough
    • Alpha-beta-gamma diversity over time
    • End-to-end with ParlGov data
    • Paper replication
    • Replicating the paper with partyscape
  • Changelog

Replicating the paper with partyscape

Source: vignettes/replication.Rmd
replication.Rmd

This vignette reproduces, in the order of appearance, every number and figure reported in main.tex (Diversity Profiles for Party Systems) using partyscape. The PCA-critique sections (§1–§3) are out of the package’s scope and use base prcomp() / lm(); every quantity those sections compare against (hill_number, hhi, enp, dominance_gap, taagepera_allik_shares) and the positive programme of §4–§6 (diversity_profile, crossings, alpha_beta_gamma, collapse_to_elections, fetch_parlgov, parlgov_seat_shares) come from the package.

Magyar’s CSV (party_seat_share_datafile_replication.csv, Harvard Dataverse DOI 10.7910/DVN/Q0OIMY) is required. Set PARTYSCAPE_MAGYAR_CSV to its path before knitting; §6 (alpha–beta) additionally needs live ParlGov access through fetch_parlgov(). Monte Carlo counts are reduced for vignette speed (the paper uses nsim=500n_\mathrm{sim} = 500; we default to 5050). Bump them back up to reproduce every reported digit exactly.

library(partyscape)
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(42)

dat <- read.csv(magyar_csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
X   <- as.matrix(dat[, 3:22])
rownames(X) <- dat$countryyear

cat(sprintf("Loaded %d country-years in %d countries (years %d-%d).\n",
            nrow(X), length(unique(dat$country)),
            min(dat$year), max(dat$year)))

§1 What PCA on sorted compositional data rediscovers

The paper’s headline: the first two PCs explain 87% of variance; Monte Carlo nulls (uniform Dirichlet, matched-N0N_0 Dirichlet, Taagepera–Allik Dirichlet) reproduce the qualitative loading shape, showing the eigenvectors are method-imposed, not substantive.

pca_real      <- prcomp(X, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
real_var      <- summary(pca_real)$importance[2, 1:5] * 100
round(real_var, 1)
cat(sprintf("PC1 + PC2 = %.1f%%\n", sum(real_var[1:2])))

Monte Carlo nulls

taagepera_allik_shares() comes from the package; the rest is base R.

n_sim    <- 50                 # bump to 500 for paper-exact numbers
D        <- ncol(X)
Ncy      <- nrow(X)
N0_real  <- rowSums(X > 0)
alpha_CH <- 39.1

sim_types <- c("Uniform Dirichlet",
               "Matched-N0 Dirichlet",
               "Taagepera-Allik Dirichlet")

sim_results <- setNames(vector("list", length(sim_types)), sim_types)
for (type in sim_types) {
  sim_results[[type]] <- list(
    var1 = numeric(n_sim), var2 = numeric(n_sim),
    L1 = matrix(0, n_sim, D), L2 = matrix(0, n_sim, D)
  )
}

for (s in seq_len(n_sim)) {
  # (A) Uniform Dirichlet, sorted
  Xu <- matrix(rexp(Ncy * D, 1), Ncy, D); Xu <- Xu / rowSums(Xu)
  Xu <- t(apply(Xu, 1, sort, decreasing = TRUE))

  # (B) Matched-N_0 Dirichlet
  Xm <- matrix(0, Ncy, D)
  for (i in seq_len(Ncy)) {
    n0 <- N0_real[i]
    d  <- rexp(n0, 1); d <- sort(d / sum(d), decreasing = TRUE)
    Xm[i, 1:n0] <- d
  }

  # (C) Taagepera-Allik (Cohen & Hanretty 2024, alpha = 39.1)
  Xt <- matrix(0, Ncy, D)
  for (i in seq_len(Ncy)) {
    n0    <- N0_real[i]
    p_loc <- taagepera_allik_shares(n0)            # <- package helper
    theta <- alpha_CH * p_loc
    d     <- rgamma(n0, shape = theta, rate = 1)
    d     <- sort(d / sum(d), decreasing = TRUE)
    Xt[i, 1:n0] <- d
  }

  for (k in seq_along(sim_types)) {
    Xs   <- switch(k, Xu, Xm, Xt)
    pp   <- prcomp(Xs, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
    sim_results[[k]]$var1[s] <- summary(pp)$importance[2, 1] * 100
    sim_results[[k]]$var2[s] <- summary(pp)$importance[2, 2] * 100
    sim_results[[k]]$L1[s, ] <- pp$rotation[, 1]
    sim_results[[k]]$L2[s, ] <- pp$rotation[, 2]
  }
}

# Sign-align: PC1 entry 1 positive, PC2 entry 1 negative
real_load <- pca_real$rotation[, 1:2]
if (real_load[1, 1] < 0) real_load[, 1] <- -real_load[, 1]
if (real_load[1, 2] > 0) real_load[, 2] <- -real_load[, 2]
for (type in sim_types) for (s in seq_len(n_sim)) {
  if (sim_results[[type]]$L1[s, 1] < 0)
    sim_results[[type]]$L1[s, ] <- -sim_results[[type]]$L1[s, ]
  if (sim_results[[type]]$L2[s, 1] > 0)
    sim_results[[type]]$L2[s, ] <- -sim_results[[type]]$L2[s, ]
}

mc_diag <- data.frame(
  null = sim_types,
  var1 = sapply(sim_results, function(x) mean(x$var1)),
  sd1  = sapply(sim_results, function(x) sd(x$var1)),
  var2 = sapply(sim_results, function(x) mean(x$var2)),
  sd2  = sapply(sim_results, function(x) sd(x$var2)),
  abs_r_PC1 = sapply(sim_results, function(x)
    mean(abs(apply(x$L1, 1, cor, real_load[, 1])))),
  abs_r_PC2 = sapply(sim_results, function(x)
    mean(abs(apply(x$L2, 1, cor, real_load[, 2]))))
)
round(mc_diag[, -1], 3)

Paper’s reference numbers: uniform null ≈ 60.3% / 18.6% variance, |r|≈0.88|r| \approx 0.88 (PC1) / 0.660.66 (PC2); matched-N0N_0 and Taagepera–Allik nulls correlate near 1 with the real loadings.

Figure 1 — loading envelopes 

df_fig1 <- data.frame()
df_fig1 <- rbind(df_fig1, data.frame(
  party   = rep(1:D, 2),
  loading = c(real_load[, 1], real_load[, 2]),
  lower   = NA, upper = NA,
  source  = "Real data (Magyar)",
  pc      = rep(c("PC1", "PC2"), each = D)
))
for (type in sim_types) {
  m1  <- colMeans(sim_results[[type]]$L1)
  ci1 <- apply(sim_results[[type]]$L1, 2, quantile, c(0.025, 0.975))
  m2  <- colMeans(sim_results[[type]]$L2)
  ci2 <- apply(sim_results[[type]]$L2, 2, quantile, c(0.025, 0.975))
  df_fig1 <- rbind(df_fig1, data.frame(
    party = rep(1:D, 2),
    loading = c(m1, m2),
    lower   = c(ci1[1, ], ci2[1, ]),
    upper   = c(ci1[2, ], ci2[2, ]),
    source  = paste0(type, " (95% CI)"),
    pc      = rep(c("PC1", "PC2"), each = D)
  ))
}

ggplot(df_fig1, aes(party, loading, colour = source, fill = source)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~pc) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = partyscape_palette(4)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values   = partyscape_palette(4), guide = "none") +
  labs(x = "Party rank", y = "Loading weight", colour = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "vertical")

§2 PC1 and the effective number of parties

Scalars come from partyscape. The Magyar columns are already sorted, so X[, 1] is x(1)x_{(1)}.

hh_real  <- hhi(X)                    # HHI per row
enp_real <- enp(X)                    # 1 / HHI
z_top    <- X[, 1] + X[, 2]
z_gap    <- X[, 1] - X[, 2]           # same as dominance_gap(X)
tail_sq  <- rowSums(X[, 3:D]^2)

pc1 <- pca_real$x[, 1]
c(R2_PC1_HH  = summary(lm(pc1 ~ hh_real))$r.squared,
  R2_PC1_ENP = summary(lm(pc1 ~ enp_real))$r.squared,
  R2_PC1_top = summary(lm(pc1 ~ z_top))$r.squared,
  r_PC1_HH   = cor(pc1, hh_real),
  r_PC1_ENP  = cor(pc1, enp_real)) |> round(4)

HH variance decomposition (§2.2) 

c(var_ztop2 = var(z_top^2 / 2),
  var_zgap2 = var(z_gap^2 / 2),
  var_tail  = var(tail_sq))           # paper: 0.0123 / 0.0005 / 0.0005

Closure constraint and sorting-induced variance concentration (§A3) 

cov_X  <- cov(X)
cat(sprintf("max |rowSums(Sigma)| = %.3e  (closure)\n",
            max(abs(rowSums(cov_X)))))
col_vars <- apply(X, 2, var)
round(col_vars[1:5], 4)
cat(sprintf("Var(x_(1)) / total = %.3f  (paper: 0.453)\n",
            col_vars[1] / sum(col_vars)))

Two-dimensional sub-PCA (§2.2)

Sub-PCA on (ztop,zgap)(z_\text{top}, z_\text{gap}) alone predicts the full-PCA PC1 at R2=0.991R^2 = 0.991 and PC2 at R2=0.993R^2 = 0.993.

Z     <- cbind(z_top, z_gap)
pca_Z <- prcomp(Z, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
sub   <- pca_Z$x
r2_pc1_sub <- max(summary(lm(pc1 ~ sub[, 1]))$r.squared,
                  summary(lm(pc1 ~ sub[, 2]))$r.squared)
r2_pc2_sub <- max(summary(lm(pca_real$x[, 2] ~ sub[, 1]))$r.squared,
                  summary(lm(pca_real$x[, 2] ~ sub[, 2]))$r.squared)
c(R2_full_PC1_from_2D = r2_pc1_sub,
  R2_full_PC2_from_2D = r2_pc2_sub) |> round(4)

§3 PC2 and the dominance gap

Σ₁₂ and the (A,B,C)(A, B, C) triple (§A4) 

S11 <- cov_X[1, 1]; S22 <- cov_X[2, 2]; S12 <- cov_X[1, 2]
A_var <- S11 + 2 * S12 + S22          # Var(z_top)
B_var <- S11 - 2 * S12 + S22          # Var(z_gap)
C_cov <- S11 - S22                    # Cov(z_top, z_gap)
c(Sigma11 = S11, Sigma22 = S22, Sigma12 = S12,
  A = A_var, B = B_var, C = C_cov,
  cor_top_gap = C_cov / sqrt(A_var * B_var)) |> round(5)

The paper reports Σ12=+0.00413\Sigma_{12} = +0.00413, A=0.0265A = 0.0265, B=0.010B = 0.010.

Eigenvectors of ΣZ\Sigma_Z — (0.96,0.28)(0.96, 0.28) and (−0.28,0.96)(-0.28, 0.96) 

eig <- eigen(cov(Z))
V   <- eig$vectors
# Sign-align so the heavy component of each eigenvector is positive
for (k in 1:2) {
  heavy <- which.max(abs(V[, k]))
  if (V[heavy, k] < 0) V[, k] <- -V[, k]
}
round(V, 3)
round(eig$values, 5)

PC2 vs. the NaN_a family (§3.2)

hill_number() does the work; correlations should sit below 0.10 for a≤3a \le 3 and rise to ≈0.20\approx 0.20 at a=10a = 10.

pc2 <- pca_real$x[, 2]
q_probe <- c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 5, 10)
Na_mat <- sapply(q_probe, function(q) apply(X, 1, hill_number, q = q))
colnames(Na_mat) <- paste0("N_", q_probe)
round(cor(pc2, Na_mat), 3)

cat(sprintf("R^2(PC2 ~ gap)         = %.3f\n",
            summary(lm(pc2 ~ dominance_gap(X)))$r.squared))
cat(sprintf("cor(ENP, z_top)        = %.3f  (paper: 0.96)\n",
            cor(enp_real, z_top)))

Figure: NaN_a level sets for a 3-party system

Every scalar on the plot is a hill_number() call.

Na_3p <- function(x1, x2, a) hill_number(c(x1, x2, 1 - x1 - x2), a)

x1_ref <- 0.45; x2_ref <- 0.30
a_grid <- c(0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 10)
ref_Na <- sapply(a_grid, function(a) Na_3p(x1_ref, x2_ref, a))

g <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(0.34, 0.65, length.out = 200),
                 x2 = seq(0.05, 0.50, length.out = 200))
g$x3 <- 1 - g$x1 - g$x2
g    <- g[g$x1 >= g$x2 & g$x2 >= g$x3 & g$x3 >= 0, ]

levels_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(a_grid), function(k) {
  a <- a_grid[k]
  data.frame(g, Na = mapply(Na_3p, g$x1, g$x2, MoreArgs = list(a = a)),
             a = factor(paste0("a = ", a), levels = paste0("a = ", a_grid)))
}))

ggplot(levels_df, aes(x1, x2, z = Na, colour = a)) +
  geom_contour(aes(group = a),
               breaks = ref_Na[match(as.numeric(sub("a = ", "", levels(levels_df$a))),
                                     a_grid)],
               linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = data.frame(x1 = x1_ref, x2 = x2_ref),
             aes(x1, x2), inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = partyscape_palette(length(a_grid))) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(x = expression(x[(1)]), y = expression(x[(2)]),
       colour = "level set",
       title = "Level sets of N_a through A = (0.45, 0.30, 0.25)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

§4 Diversity profiles — UK 1997 vs. Germany 2009 

uk97  <- X["United Kingdom_1997", ]
ger09 <- X["Germany_2009", ]

scalars <- data.frame(
  N0     = c(sum(uk97 > 0),           sum(ger09 > 0)),
  ENP    = c(enp(uk97),               enp(ger09)),
  N_inf  = c(1 / max(uk97),           1 / max(ger09)),
  gap    = c(dominance_gap(uk97),     dominance_gap(ger09)),
  topTwo = c(top_two(uk97),           top_two(ger09))
)
rownames(scalars) <- c("UK 1997", "Germany 2009")
round(scalars, 3)

dp_pair <- diversity_profile(rbind(`UK 1997` = uk97,
                                   `Germany 2009` = ger09),
                             q = seq(0, 8, 0.02))
autoplot(dp_pair) +
  labs(title = "UK 1997 (steep — concentrated) vs. Germany 2009 (flat — balanced)")

§6 Crossing diversity profiles

16-country-year illustrative sample (§6 paragraph 1) 

q_fine  <- seq(0, 10, 0.02)
all_enp <- enp(X)
all_gap <- dominance_gap(X)

select_16cy_kmeans <- function(enp_vec, gap_vec, k = 16, seed = 42) {
  feats <- cbind(scale(enp_vec), scale(gap_vec))
  set.seed(seed)
  km <- kmeans(feats, centers = k, nstart = 50, iter.max = 100)
  sel <- integer(k)
  for (cl in seq_len(k)) {
    members <- which(km$cluster == cl)
    d <- rowSums(sweep(feats[members, , drop = FALSE], 2,
                       km$centers[cl, ])^2)
    sel[cl] <- members[which.min(d)]
  }
  sort(unique(sel))
}
sel_16 <- select_16cy_kmeans(all_enp, all_gap)
data.frame(country_year = rownames(X)[sel_16],
           ENP = round(all_enp[sel_16], 2),
           gap = round(all_gap[sel_16], 3))
M16    <- X[sel_16, , drop = FALSE]
cr_16  <- crossings(M16, q = q_fine, method = "signchange")
n_cross_16 <- sum(cr_16$pairs$n_crossings > 0)
cat(sprintf("16-CY sample: %d of %d pairs cross (%.1f%%)  [paper: 44/120 = 36.7%%]\n",
            n_cross_16, nrow(cr_16$pairs),
            100 * n_cross_16 / nrow(cr_16$pairs)))

Table 1 — virtually-identical-ENP pairs 

tbl <- cr_16$pairs
tbl$system_A <- rownames(M16)[tbl$i]
tbl$system_B <- rownames(M16)[tbl$j]
tbl$ENP_A    <- round(all_enp[sel_16][tbl$i], 2)
tbl$ENP_B    <- round(all_enp[sel_16][tbl$j], 2)
tbl$dENP     <- round(abs(all_enp[sel_16][tbl$i] -
                           all_enp[sel_16][tbl$j]), 2)
tbl$dgap     <- round(abs(all_gap[sel_16][tbl$i] -
                           all_gap[sel_16][tbl$j]), 3)
tbl$N0_A     <- rowSums(M16 > 0)[tbl$i]
tbl$N0_B     <- rowSums(M16 > 0)[tbl$j]

cross_pairs <- tbl[tbl$n_crossings > 0, ]
cross_pairs <- cross_pairs[order(cross_pairs$dENP), ]
head(cross_pairs[, c("system_A", "system_B", "N0_A", "N0_B",
                     "ENP_A", "ENP_B", "dENP", "dgap",
                     "n_crossings", "first_q")], 10)

Stratified-redraw robustness (§6 paragraph 2)

100 draws on a 4$$4 (ENP, gap) quartile grid.

stratified_redraw_rate <- function(enp_vec, gap_vec, n_draws = 100,
                                   seed = 2026) {
  enp_q <- quantile(enp_vec, seq(0, 1, length.out = 5))
  gap_q <- quantile(gap_vec, seq(0, 1, length.out = 5))
  set.seed(seed)
  rates <- numeric(n_draws)
  for (d in seq_len(n_draws)) {
    sel <- integer(0)
    for (i in 1:4) for (j in 1:4) {
      in_cell <- enp_vec >= enp_q[i] & enp_vec <= enp_q[i + 1] &
                 gap_vec >= gap_q[j] & gap_vec <= gap_q[j + 1]
      idx <- which(in_cell)
      if (length(idx)) sel <- c(sel, sample(idx, 1))
    }
    if (length(sel) < 16) {
      sel <- c(sel, sample(setdiff(seq_along(enp_vec), sel),
                           16 - length(sel)))
    } else if (length(sel) > 16) sel <- sample(sel, 16)
    cr <- crossings(X[sel, , drop = FALSE], q = q_fine,
                    method = "signchange")
    rates[d] <- mean(cr$pairs$n_crossings > 0)
  }
  rates
}

redraw <- stratified_redraw_rate(all_enp, all_gap, n_draws = 100)
c(mean     = mean(redraw),
  p5       = quantile(redraw, 0.05),
  p95      = quantile(redraw, 0.95),
  min      = min(redraw),
  max      = max(redraw)) |> round(3)

Paper: mean 33%, 5th–95th percentile 22%–44%.

Full-dataset scan — all 675 country-years, |ΔENP| < 0.5

We compute the full profile matrix once with diversity_profile() and pull the wide attribute for fast sign-change scanning.

dp_full <- diversity_profile(X, q = q_fine)
prof_mat <- attr(dp_full, "wide")

count_crossings_row <- function(pi, pj) {
  d  <- pi - pj
  nz <- d != 0
  d  <- d[nz]
  if (length(d) < 2) return(list(n = 0L, first = NA_real_))
  s  <- sign(d)
  ch <- which(diff(s) != 0)
  list(n = length(ch),
       first = if (length(ch)) q_fine[nz][ch[1]] else NA_real_)
}

pairs_df <- data.frame()
for (i in 1:(Ncy - 1L)) {
  cand <- (i + 1L):Ncy
  keep <- abs(all_enp[i] - all_enp[cand]) < 0.5
  cand <- cand[keep]
  for (j in cand) {
    r <- count_crossings_row(prof_mat[i, ], prof_mat[j, ])
    pairs_df <- rbind(pairs_df, data.frame(
      i = i, j = j,
      dENP = abs(all_enp[i] - all_enp[j]),
      dgap = abs(all_gap[i] - all_gap[j]),
      n_cross = r$n,
      first_q = r$first
    ))
  }
}

cat(sprintf("Near-ENP pairs (|dENP| < 0.5): %d  [paper: 53,861]\n",
            nrow(pairs_df)))
cat(sprintf("Pairs with >=1 crossing:        %d (%.1f%%)  [paper: 66%%]\n",
            sum(pairs_df$n_cross > 0),
            100 * mean(pairs_df$n_cross > 0)))
cat(sprintf("Mean first-crossing q:          %.2f  [paper: 1.98]\n",
            mean(pairs_df$first_q, na.rm = TRUE)))

for (b in list(c(0, 0.05), c(0.05, 0.10), c(0.10, 0.15), c(0.15, 1))) {
  m <- pairs_df$dgap >= b[1] & pairs_df$dgap < b[2]
  cat(sprintf("  |dgap| in [%.2f, %.2f): n = %5d, rate = %.3f\n",
              b[1], b[2], sum(m), mean(pairs_df$n_cross[m] > 0)))
}

Paper’s ladder: 52% → 79% across the |Δzgap||\Delta z_{\mathrm{gap}}| bands.

France 2007 vs. Spain 2000 (sanity check) 

fr <- X["France_2007", ]; sp <- X["Spain_2000", ]
cat(sprintf("France 2007: x(1)=%.4f x(2)=%.4f gap=%.4f N0=%d ENP=%.3f N_inf=%.3f\n",
            fr[1], fr[2], fr[1] - fr[2], sum(fr > 0), enp(fr), 1 / max(fr)))
cat(sprintf("Spain  2000: x(1)=%.4f x(2)=%.4f gap=%.4f N0=%d ENP=%.3f N_inf=%.3f\n",
            sp[1], sp[2], sp[1] - sp[2], sum(sp > 0), enp(sp), 1 / max(sp)))

cr_FS <- crossings(fr, sp, q = q_fine)
cat(sprintf("Crossings at q = %s\n",
            paste(round(cr_FS$locations[[1]], 3), collapse = ", ")))

Figure 2 — case-study profile pairs

Netherlands 1982 vs. Sweden 2002 (two crossings); UK 2005 vs. Greece 2007 (single crossing); Italy 2006 vs. Portugal 2005 (non-crossing, uniform dominance).

case_pairs <- list(
  c("Netherlands_1982",     "Sweden_2002"),
  c("United Kingdom_2005",  "Greece_2007"),
  c("Italy_2006",           "Portugal_2005")
)
for (pp in case_pairs) {
  cr <- crossings(X[pp[1], ], X[pp[2], ], q = q_fine)
  cat(sprintf("\n%s vs %s\n  ENP: %.2f vs %.2f  |  N_0: %d vs %d  |  N_inf: %.2f vs %.2f\n  crossings at q = %s\n",
              pp[1], pp[2],
              enp(X[pp[1], ]), enp(X[pp[2], ]),
              sum(X[pp[1], ] > 0), sum(X[pp[2], ] > 0),
              1 / max(X[pp[1], ]), 1 / max(X[pp[2], ]),
              if (cr$pairs$n_crossings == 0) "(none)"
              else paste(round(cr$locations[[1]], 3), collapse = ", ")))
}

plots <- lapply(case_pairs, function(pp) {
  cr <- crossings(X[pp[1], ], X[pp[2], ], q = q_fine)
  autoplot(cr) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed",
                        colour = "grey50") +
    ggplot2::labs(title = paste(pp[1], "vs", pp[2]))
})
for (g in plots) print(g)

§7 Alpha–beta decomposition

Sorted (Magyar) representation

alpha_beta_gamma() with years = ... auto-collapses repeated consecutive rows via collapse_to_elections() and weights by term length.

countries <- unique(dat$country)
q_beta    <- c(0, 1, 2, 5)

beta_sorted <- do.call(rbind, lapply(countries, function(cc) {
  idx <- which(dat$country == cc)
  if (length(idx) < 3) return(NULL)
  bd  <- alpha_beta_gamma(X[idx, , drop = FALSE],
                          q = q_beta, years = dat$year[idx],
                          collapse = TRUE)
  data.frame(country = cc, q = bd$q, alpha = bd$alpha,
             beta = bd$beta, gamma = bd$gamma, T = bd$T)
}))

# Tidy wide table, ordered by beta at q = 0 (Figure 3's ordering)
wide_sorted <- reshape(
  beta_sorted[, c("country", "q", "beta")],
  idvar = "country", timevar = "q", direction = "wide"
)
names(wide_sorted) <- c("country", paste0("beta_q", q_beta))
wide_sorted <- wide_sorted[order(-wide_sorted$beta_q0), ]
rownames(wide_sorted) <- NULL
knitr::kable(wide_sorted, digits = 3,
             caption = "Sorted-data beta by order (paper's Figure 3b).")

Key countries cited in the text:

for (cc in c("Greece", "Austria", "Italy", "Finland", "United Kingdom")) {
  row <- wide_sorted[wide_sorted$country == cc, ]
  cat(sprintf("  %-15s  beta_q0 = %.3f  beta_q2 = %.3f  beta_q5 = %.3f\n",
              cc, row$beta_q0, row$beta_q2, row$beta_q5))
}

Unit-test (disjoint communities should give β2=2\beta_2 = 2):

disjoint <- rbind(c(rep(0.1, 10), rep(0, 10)),
                  c(rep(0, 10), 0.75, 0.25, rep(0, 8)))
tst <- alpha_beta_gamma(disjoint, q = 2)
stopifnot(abs(tst$beta - 2) < 1e-8)
cat(sprintf("Disjoint test: gamma=%.3f  alpha=%.3f  beta=%.3f  (expect 5.517, 2.759, 2.000)\n",
            tst$gamma, tst$alpha, tst$beta))

Unsorted (ParlGov) representation 

parlgov_names <- c("Austria", "Belgium", "Denmark", "Finland", "France",
                   "Germany", "Greece", "Iceland", "Ireland", "Italy",
                   "Luxembourg", "Netherlands", "Norway", "Portugal",
                   "Spain", "Sweden", "United Kingdom")

pg <- tryCatch(fetch_parlgov(), error = function(e) {
  message("fetch_parlgov() failed (", conditionMessage(e),
          "); falling back to local CSV.")
  read.csv(parlgov_local, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
})

# The live endpoint uses full country names ("Germany"); the Magyar
# replication CSV uses 3-letter ISO codes ("DEU"). Normalize to full
# names so the rest of the chunk works regardless of source.
iso_to_name <- c(AUT = "Austria", BEL = "Belgium", DNK = "Denmark",
                 FIN = "Finland", FRA = "France", DEU = "Germany",
                 GRC = "Greece",  ISL = "Iceland", IRL = "Ireland",
                 ITA = "Italy",   LUX = "Luxembourg",
                 NLD = "Netherlands", NOR = "Norway", PRT = "Portugal",
                 ESP = "Spain",   SWE = "Sweden",
                 GBR = "United Kingdom")
if (all(nchar(unique(pg$country)) == 3L)) {
  pg$country <- unname(iso_to_name[pg$country])
  pg <- pg[!is.na(pg$country), ]
}

pg  <- pg[pg$country %in% parlgov_names, ]
pg  <- pg[!is.na(pg$seats), ]
pg$year <- as.integer(substr(pg$date, 1, 4))
pg  <- pg[pg$year >= min(dat$year) & pg$year <= max(dat$year), ]

shares <- parlgov_seat_shares(pg,
                              countries = parlgov_names,
                              years     = min(dat$year):max(dat$year),
                              type      = "parliament")
dim(shares)
country_vec <- attr(shares, "country")
year_vec    <- attr(shares, "year")

beta_pg <- do.call(rbind, lapply(parlgov_names, function(cc) {
  keep <- country_vec == cc
  if (sum(keep) < 3) return(NULL)
  mat <- shares[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  # drop party columns that are zero throughout this country's history
  mat <- mat[, colSums(mat) > 0, drop = FALSE]
  bd  <- alpha_beta_gamma(mat, q = q_beta, years = year_vec[keep])
  data.frame(country = cc, q = bd$q, alpha = bd$alpha,
             beta = bd$beta, gamma = bd$gamma, T = bd$T)
}))

wide_pg <- reshape(
  beta_pg[, c("country", "q", "beta")],
  idvar = "country", timevar = "q", direction = "wide"
)
names(wide_pg) <- c("country", paste0("beta_q", q_beta))
wide_pg <- wide_pg[order(-wide_pg$beta_q0), ]
rownames(wide_pg) <- NULL
knitr::kable(wide_pg, digits = 3,
             caption = "Unsorted (ParlGov) beta by order (paper's Figure 3).")

Italy’s headline numbers:

beta_pg[beta_pg$country == "Italy", ]

Paper reports β0=3.87\beta_0 = 3.87, β2=2.35\beta_2 = 2.35, β5=2.09\beta_5 = 2.09.

Sorted-vs-unsorted gap at q = 2 (Figure 3c) 

cmp <- merge(beta_sorted[, c("country", "q", "beta")],
             beta_pg[,     c("country", "q", "beta")],
             by = c("country", "q"),
             suffixes = c(".sorted", ".parlgov"))
cmp_q2 <- cmp[cmp$q == 2, ]
cmp_q2 <- cmp_q2[order(cmp_q2$beta.parlgov), ]
cmp_q2$country <- factor(cmp_q2$country, levels = cmp_q2$country)

ggplot(cmp_q2) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = beta.sorted, xend = beta.parlgov,
                   y = country, yend = country),
               colour = "grey70") +
  geom_point(aes(x = beta.sorted, y = country,
                 colour = "sorted (Magyar)"), size = 2) +
  geom_point(aes(x = beta.parlgov, y = country,
                 colour = "unsorted (ParlGov)"), size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = partyscape_palette(2)) +
  labs(x = expression(beta(q == 2)), y = NULL, colour = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

Continuous β(q) profile (Figure 4) 

q_grid <- seq(0, 5, 0.1)

profile_one <- function(mat, years) {
  bd <- alpha_beta_gamma(mat, q = q_grid, years = years)
  bd$beta
}

bp_sorted <- do.call(rbind, lapply(countries, function(cc) {
  idx <- which(dat$country == cc)
  if (length(idx) < 3) return(NULL)
  data.frame(country = cc, q = q_grid,
             beta = profile_one(X[idx, , drop = FALSE], dat$year[idx]),
             panel = "Sorted (Magyar)")
}))

bp_pg <- do.call(rbind, lapply(parlgov_names, function(cc) {
  keep <- country_vec == cc
  if (sum(keep) < 3) return(NULL)
  mat <- shares[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  mat <- mat[, colSums(mat) > 0, drop = FALSE]
  data.frame(country = cc, q = q_grid,
             beta = profile_one(mat, year_vec[keep]),
             panel = "Unsorted (ParlGov)")
}))

bp <- rbind(bp_sorted, bp_pg)
hi <- c("Italy", "Belgium", "Greece", "Austria")
bp$colour <- ifelse(bp$country %in% hi, bp$country, "(other)")

ggplot(bp, aes(q, beta, group = country, colour = colour)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    Italy   = partyscape_palette(4)[1],
    Belgium = partyscape_palette(4)[2],
    Greece  = partyscape_palette(4)[3],
    Austria = partyscape_palette(4)[4],
    `(other)` = "grey75")) +
  labs(x = "order q", y = expression(beta(q)), colour = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

Summary of what’s replicated

Paper section Key claim Replicated via
§1 PC1/PC2 variance = 70.6% / 16.5% prcomp()
§1 Three Monte Carlo nulls, |r||r| with real loadings taagepera_allik_shares() + prcomp()
§2 R2(PC1,HH)=0.96R^2(\text{PC1}, \text{HH}) = 0.96; (A,B,C)(A, B, C) triple hhi(), enp() + lm()
§2 2×2 sub-PCA predicts full PC1 at R2=0.991R^2 = 0.991 prcomp() on (ztop,zgap)(z_\text{top}, z_\text{gap})
§3 |r(PC2,Na)|<0.10|r(\text{PC2}, N_a)| < 0.10 for a≤3a \le 3 hill_number()
§3 R2(PC2,gap)≈0.96R^2(\text{PC2}, \text{gap}) \approx 0.96 dominance_gap() + lm()
§4 UK 1997 / Germany 2009 anchor values diversity_profile(), hill_number(), enp(), scalars
§6 16-CY crossing rate ≈ 37% crossings()
§6 Stratified-redraw 22–44% crossings()
§6 Full-dataset scan: 66% crossing, q‾1≈1.98\bar{q}_1 \approx 1.98 diversity_profile() + sign-change scan
§6 France/Spain, NL/SE, UK/GR, IT/PT case pairs crossings()
§7 Per-country β\beta at q∈{0,1,2,5}q \in \{0,1,2,5\}, sorted + unsorted alpha_beta_gamma(), collapse_to_elections(), fetch_parlgov(), parlgov_seat_shares()
§7 Italy β0=3.87\beta_0 = 3.87, β2=2.35\beta_2 = 2.35, β5=2.09\beta_5 = 2.09 alpha_beta_gamma() on ParlGov
§7 Sorted ≤\le unsorted at every qq same
§7 Disjoint-communities sanity: β2=2\beta_2 = 2 alpha_beta_gamma()

For paper-exact headline numbers, bump n_sim back to 500 in §1.

On this page

Developed by Marcel Neunhoeffer.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
